_meta.description: KV Cache Eviction 평가용 벤치마크 프롬프트

perplexity._description: 언어 모델링 품질 평가. 다양한 도메인 텍스트의 continuation 품질을 측정한다. 각 프롬프트로 N 토큰을 생성하고, baseline(full cache) vs eviction의 top_logits 분포를 비교한다.

prompts.description: 문학 작품 스타일 — 서사적 묘사와 캐릭터 심리
prompts.prompt: It was a bright cold day in April, and the clocks were striking thirteen. The street was deserted except for a few figures hurrying along the pavement, their coat collars turned up against the wind that swept down from the grey towers.

prompts.description: 백과사전 스타일 — 과학적 사실과 역사적 맥락
prompts.prompt: The theory of plate tectonics describes the large-scale motion of seven major plates and many minor plates of Earth's lithosphere. The concept was first proposed in 1912 by Alfred Wegener as continental drift, but it was not until the 1960s that a comprehensive theory emerged.

prompts.description: 기술 문서 스타일 — 알고리즘과 자료구조 설명
prompts.prompt: A hash table is a data structure that implements an associative array, mapping keys to values. It uses a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets or slots, from which the desired value can be found. Ideally, the hash function will assign each key to a unique bucket.

prompts.description: 대화체 스타일 — 일상적 설명과 의견 표현
prompts.prompt: So the thing about learning a new language is that everyone tells you to just immerse yourself, but nobody really explains what that means in practice. I tried watching movies without subtitles for a month, and honestly, it was frustrating at first because I could barely catch every other word.

prompts.description: 뉴스 기사 스타일 — 객관적 보도와 인용
prompts.prompt: Scientists at the European Organization for Nuclear Research announced preliminary results from a new series of experiments that could reshape our understanding of fundamental physics. The findings, which have not yet been peer reviewed, suggest the existence of previously undetected interactions between subatomic particles.

niah._description: Needle-in-a-Haystack 평가. 배경 텍스트 속에 특정 정보(needle)를 삽입하고, eviction 후에도 모델이 이를 검색할 수 있는지 측정한다. 프롬프트 마지막에 질문을 배치하여 모델이 needle 정보를 출력하도록 유도한다.
niah._usage: 프롬프트를 구성할 때 filler_blocks에서 배경 텍스트를 선택하고, needle을 원하는 depth에 삽입한 뒤, question으로 끝낸다.
niah._evaluation: 생성된 텍스트에 expected_answer가 포함되어 있으면 success=1, 아니면 0

filler_blocks.text: Urban planning involves the technical and political process of developing land use strategies. Modern cities face challenges including traffic congestion, housing affordability, and environmental sustainability. Planners must balance economic development with community needs, often working with zoning regulations, transportation networks, and public space design to create livable environments.

The ocean covers approximately seventy-one percent of the Earth's surface and contains an estimated ninety-seven percent of the planet's water. Marine ecosystems range from shallow coastal waters to the deep ocean floor, each hosting unique communities of organisms. Coral reefs, often called the rainforests of the sea, support roughly twenty-five percent of all marine species despite covering less than one percent of the ocean floor.

The development of Western music can be traced through several distinct periods, each characterized by particular styles and compositional techniques. The Baroque period saw the emergence of opera, the concerto, and the sonata. The Classical era brought greater emphasis on clarity, balance, and formal structure, exemplified by the works of Haydn and Mozart.

Sustainable agriculture practices aim to meet society's food needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. Techniques include crop rotation, which helps maintain soil fertility and reduces pest buildup, and integrated pest management, which minimizes the use of chemical pesticides through biological controls and careful monitoring of pest populations.

The Milky Way galaxy is a barred spiral galaxy with an estimated diameter between one hundred thousand and one hundred eighty thousand light-years. It contains between one hundred billion and four hundred billion stars, along with at least that many planets. Our solar system is located about twenty-six thousand light-years from the galactic center, in the Orion Arm.

Archaeological methods have evolved significantly since the field's origins in the nineteenth century. Modern archaeology employs remote sensing technologies, including satellite imagery and ground-penetrating radar, to locate buried structures without excavation. Radiocarbon dating, developed in the late 1940s, revolutionized the ability to determine the age of organic materials up to roughly fifty thousand years old.

Supply and demand form the foundational model for understanding market economies. When the price of a good increases, the quantity demanded typically decreases, while the quantity supplied increases. Market equilibrium occurs at the price where the quantity demanded equals the quantity supplied. External factors such as government regulations, technological changes, and consumer preferences can shift both curves.

Cognitive psychology studies mental processes including attention, memory, perception, and problem solving. Working memory, a system for temporarily holding and manipulating information, plays a central role in reasoning and decision making. Research has shown that the capacity of working memory is limited, typically allowing the simultaneous processing of approximately four chunks of information.

niah.assembly_instructions: 프롬프트 조합 방법: (1) depth_ratio에 따라 filler_blocks를 needle 앞뒤로 배분 (2) needle_text를 해당 위치에 삽입 (3) 나머지 filler로 원하는 길이를 채운 뒤 (4) question을 마지막에 배치. 예: depth=0.25, 길이=8 blocks → 앞 2 blocks + needle + 뒤 6 blocks + question.

qa._description: LongBench 스타일 QA 평가. 문서+질문 형식으로 다양한 태스크 유형에서 eviction 영향을 측정한다.
qa._evaluation: generated text에서 answer span을 추출하고 expected_answer와 F1/EM 계산

single_doc_qa.document: The Panama Canal is an artificial waterway in Panama that connects the Atlantic Ocean with the Pacific Ocean. The canal cuts across the Isthmus of Panama and is a conduit for maritime trade. Construction of the canal was one of the largest and most difficult engineering projects ever undertaken. France began work on the canal in 1881 but stopped due to engineering problems and high mortality among workers. The United States took over the project in 1904 and opened the canal on August 15, 1914. One of the largest and most difficult engineering projects ever undertaken, the canal had an enormous impact on shipping between the two oceans, eliminating the need to navigate the long and treacherous route around the southern tip of South America via the Drake Passage. The canal uses a system of locks that lift ships up to Gatun Lake, an artificial lake created to reduce the amount of excavation work required, and then lower them on the other side. The original locks are 33.5 meters wide. A third, wider lane of locks was opened on June 26, 2016, allowing larger ships known as New Panamax vessels to pass through.
single_doc_qa.question: When did the United States take over the Panama Canal project and when was it opened?
single_doc_qa.expected_answer: The United States took over the project in 1904 and opened the canal on August 15, 1914.

single_doc_qa.document: Photosynthesis is a process used by plants and other organisms to convert light energy into chemical energy that can be stored and later released to fuel the organism's activities. In most cases, oxygen is also released as a waste product. Most plants, algae, and cyanobacteria perform photosynthesis, which is largely responsible for producing and maintaining the oxygen content of Earth's atmosphere. Photosynthesis occurs in two main stages: the light-dependent reactions and the Calvin cycle. During the light-dependent reactions, which take place in the thylakoid membranes of the chloroplast, water molecules are split using light energy, producing oxygen, ATP, and NADPH. The Calvin cycle, which takes place in the stroma of the chloroplast, uses the ATP and NADPH produced in the light reactions to fix carbon dioxide into three-carbon sugars. These sugars are then used to make glucose and other organic molecules. The overall equation for photosynthesis is: 6CO2 + 6H2O + light energy produces C6H12O6 + 6O2.
single_doc_qa.question: Where do the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis take place and what do they produce?
single_doc_qa.expected_answer: The light-dependent reactions take place in the thylakoid membranes of the chloroplast, producing oxygen, ATP, and NADPH.

single_doc_qa.document: The Renaissance was a cultural movement that began in Italy during the late 14th century and later spread throughout Europe. It marked the transition from the medieval period to modernity, spanning roughly the 14th through 17th centuries. The movement began in Florence, where wealthy patrons such as the Medici family funded artists, scholars, and architects. Key figures of the Renaissance include Leonardo da Vinci, who was known for paintings such as the Mona Lisa and The Last Supper as well as his scientific investigations; Michelangelo, who created the ceiling frescoes of the Sistine Chapel and the sculpture David; and Raphael, whose School of Athens exemplified the ideals of the High Renaissance. The Renaissance also saw major advances in science, with Copernicus proposing a heliocentric model of the solar system and Galileo improving the telescope to make astronomical observations. The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440 played a critical role in spreading Renaissance ideas across Europe by making books more accessible to a wider audience.
single_doc_qa.question: Who funded the artists in Florence during the Renaissance and name three key figures of the movement.
single_doc_qa.expected_answer: The Medici family funded artists in Florence. Three key figures were Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Raphael.

summarization.document: A new study published in the journal Nature Climate Change has found that global ice loss has accelerated significantly over the past three decades. Researchers analyzed satellite data from 1994 to 2017 and found that Earth lost 28 trillion tonnes of ice during this period. The rate of ice loss increased from 0.8 trillion tonnes per year in the 1990s to 1.3 trillion tonnes per year by 2017. The greatest losses occurred in the Arctic sea ice, Antarctic ice shelves, and mountain glaciers. The study found that ice loss from the Antarctic and Greenland ice sheets has increased sixfold since the 1990s, now matching the worst-case scenarios predicted by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change. The researchers warn that for every centimeter of sea level rise, approximately one million people in low-lying coastal areas are at risk of displacement. The study emphasizes the urgent need for immediate action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to limit future ice loss and its cascading effects on global sea levels, weather patterns, and ecosystems.
summarization.question: Summarize the key findings of this study in two to three sentences.
summarization.expected_answer: Earth lost 28 trillion tonnes of ice from 1994 to 2017, with the annual rate increasing from 0.8 to 1.3 trillion tonnes. Antarctic and Greenland ice sheet losses have increased sixfold since the 1990s, matching worst-case climate predictions.

summarization.document: The human gut microbiome consists of trillions of microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, fungi, and other microscopic organisms that reside in the digestive tract. Research over the past decade has revealed that the gut microbiome plays a far more significant role in human health than previously understood. A balanced microbiome aids in digestion, produces essential vitamins such as vitamin K and certain B vitamins, helps regulate the immune system, and may even influence mood and cognitive function through what scientists call the gut-brain axis. Disruptions to the microbiome, known as dysbiosis, have been linked to conditions ranging from inflammatory bowel disease and obesity to depression and autoimmune disorders. Factors that can disrupt the microbiome include antibiotic use, poor diet particularly one high in processed foods and low in fiber, chronic stress, and lack of sleep. Strategies to maintain a healthy microbiome include consuming a diverse diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and fermented foods, getting regular exercise, managing stress, and avoiding unnecessary antibiotic use.
summarization.question: Summarize the role of the gut microbiome and what can disrupt it.
summarization.expected_answer: The gut microbiome aids digestion, produces vitamins, regulates immunity, and influences mood via the gut-brain axis. It can be disrupted by antibiotics, poor diet, stress, and lack of sleep.

multi_hop.document:
Albert Einstein was born on March 14, 1879, in Ulm, in the Kingdom of Württemberg in the German Empire. He developed the theory of relativity, one of the two pillars of modern physics alongside quantum mechanics. His work is also known for its influence on the philosophy of science. He received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his explanation of the photoelectric effect.

The Nobel Prize in Physics is awarded annually by the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences. The first Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded in 1901 to Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen for his discovery of X-rays. The prize is presented in Stockholm, Sweden, on December 10 each year, the anniversary of Alfred Nobel's death.

Alfred Nobel was a Swedish chemist, engineer, inventor, and philanthropist. He was born on October 21, 1833, in Stockholm, Sweden. He is best known for his invention of dynamite in 1867 and for establishing the Nobel Prizes in his will. Nobel signed his last will on November 27, 1895, and died on December 10, 1896, in San Remo, Italy.
multi_hop.question: In what city is the Nobel Prize presented, and what is the connection between the date of the ceremony and Alfred Nobel?
multi_hop.expected_answer: The Nobel Prize is presented in Stockholm. The ceremony is held on December 10, the anniversary of Alfred Nobel's death in 1896.

multi_hop.document:
The Amazon River is the largest river in the world by discharge volume of water. It flows through South America, primarily through Brazil, and its basin covers approximately 7 million square kilometers. The river originates in the Andes Mountains of Peru, near the Pacific coast, and flows eastward to the Atlantic Ocean.

The Andes Mountains are the longest continental mountain range in the world, stretching over 7,000 kilometers along the western edge of South America. The range passes through seven countries: Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, Chile, and Argentina. The highest peak in the Andes is Aconcagua, which stands at 6,961 meters above sea level and is located in Argentina.

Argentina is the second-largest country in South America after Brazil. Its capital is Buenos Aires, which is one of the largest cities in the world. The country shares borders with Chile, Bolivia, Paraguay, Brazil, and Uruguay.
multi_hop.question: The Amazon River originates in a mountain range. What is the highest peak of that range and in which country is it located?
multi_hop.expected_answer: The highest peak of the Andes is Aconcagua at 6,961 meters, located in Argentina.
